title: Mission plan (plan.json)
description: Harness-neutral frozen DAG produced by PLAN/FIGHT/FREEZE and consumed by any executor adapter. This artifact is the contract between brain (planner) and hands (executor); it is pure data and must contain no harness-specific constructs. See agent-constitution.md.

run_id.description: Stable id for this mission run. Names the .mission/<run_id>/ directory. Generated by the planner (no Date.now in executor scripts; stamp here).

goal.description: The one-line human goal this mission serves.

repo.description: Path or name of the target repo. Must have a ## Agent contract.

mode.description: Mid-flight escalation routing only (constitution §4). Does not change rules or floors.

constitution_version.description: Version of agent-constitution.md governing this run. Required for evolution-loop comparability (§7).

mission_class.description: Orchestration depth (§2.4), computed at PLAN from classifier_inputs by a deterministic gate (no model call). Sizes plan-level ceremony — FIGHT, heartbeat, attended go-gate, AUDIT depth. NEVER lowers a V-class floor or skips a §3.1-mandated critic. Round UP under uncertainty; promotion mid-flight is free, demotion never silent.

classifier_inputs.description: The deterministic inputs that produced mission_class (§2.4). Recorded for audit and for classification calibration (§7).

v_max.description: Highest v_class present in the plan.

zone.description: Any node touches a declared deliverable zone (§8).

outward.description: Any node is outward-facing or irreversible.

compute_role_required.description: Coarsest role this mission needs. Bound to a physical machine at runtime via machine-profile.md. A heavy node on a light machine is deferred-with-note or dispatched cross-machine.

eta.description: ETA computed at FREEZE (ISO 8601 or relative). Recomputed during EXECUTE for late-vs-diverging discrimination (§6.3). Not a deadline.

deliverable_zones.description: Path globs (copied from repo contract) that carry the V2 categorical floor. Mirrored here so the executor is self-contained.

id.description: Unique within the plan. Referenced by deps.

instruction.description: What the executor agent is told to do for this node.

deps.description: Node ids that must complete before this node is ready. Empty = root-ready.

parallelizable.description: Planner intent that this node MAY fan out. Actual fan-out also requires a declared write_set (§6.5 blast-radius rule): read-only nodes (write_set: []) fan out freely; mutating nodes fan out only as a write-set-disjoint subset under worktree isolation.

write_set.description: Blast radius: the globs / namespaces / section-ids this node MUTATES (§6.5). Two nodes may run concurrently only if their write_sets are DISJOINT over a shared read-only context. Empty array = declares read-only (always safe to fan out). Absent = unknown, so conservative serial. The executor derives parallelizability from this; the actor reports its ACTUAL write_set on completion to calibrate the estimate (§7).

v_class.description: Verification class (§2). Planner may under-specify upward via rounding; close-time binding (§2.1) downgrades V0/V1 to V2 if no closure record is produced.

check.description: Suggested check command for V0/V1, or 'TBD'/null. NOT binding at plan time — the executor binds the concrete check at close time and records it in closure_record. Prefer a name from the repo contract's verifier registry.

ac_required.description: Whether this node must be critiqued (§3). MUST be true for V2, for outward-facing/irreversible nodes, and for the final deliverable node (floors, §3.1). May be true above the floor at planner discretion.

acceptance_criteria.description: Named, checkable conditions for done. Blockers must cite one of these by reference (§3.3).

is_sub_loop.description: If true, this node is a structured sub-loop (§6.1 tier 2): fresh-context agent per iteration, state on disk.

caps.description: Per-node overrides of constitution §6.2 defaults. Each override SHOULD carry a reason.

compute_role_required.description: Per-node compute requirement (e.g. a training node needs heavy).

is_final_deliverable.description: Marks the final assembly node. Forces ac_required=true and a critic panel (correctness + completeness lenses) plus the cold verifier (§3.4).

improve_pass.description: Run a cold-improver→actor-revision quality pass on this node's FIRST DRAFT (§3.4 improver / §3.3 revision). Applies to a-c implementation nodes (not the final deliverable, which the panel already covers). Default by mission class: M2 on (opt-out with false), M1 off (opt-in with true — flag the riskiest 1-2 impl nodes), M0 never. Quality lever, not verification.
